params.text:
Reading the situation on arrival

A written risk assessment is done in advance, but situations change. Dynamic risk assessment is the quick, ongoing judgement you make in the moment, before and during a visit. The simplest version is one question: "Does this feel safe right now?"

As you approach and enter, stay alert to what is different from usual. Notice the atmosphere, who is present, whether anyone seems agitated or under the influence, and whether your exit is clear. Trust your instincts. Feeling uneasy is information, not weakness.

  • Before you knock: is the area quiet, well lit, is anything out of place?
  • As the door opens: how are you greeted, is anyone else there, can you smell smoke, alcohol or gas?
  • Inside: keep a clear route to the door and stay aware as the visit goes on.

params.alt: Keeping in touch when working alone

params.question: Dynamic risk assessment means continuing to judge whether a situation feels safe while you are actually there, not only beforehand.

l10n.wrongAnswerMessage: Not quite. Dynamic risk assessment is exactly that ongoing, in-the-moment judgement. It works alongside the planned assessment, not instead of it.
l10n.correctAnswerMessage: Correct. Dynamic risk assessment is the ongoing, in-the-moment judgement you make on arrival and throughout a visit, on top of the planned assessment.
